summary: Add `userdata write <path> <json>` low-level CRDT escape hatch over POST /v3/userdata.write. Dry-run by default; requires --apply to fire; path must start with users/.
reason: The CLI calls userdata.write internally (snooze, archive, reminders) but exposed no generic escape hatch for paths the typed commands don't cover. Useful for agent workflows and forward-compat. Uses a dedicated --apply gate, NOT the global --yes, because --yes auto-trues under --agent (root.go PersistentPreRunE) and would make a raw CRDT write auto-fire in agent mode.
validated_outcome: userdata_write_test.go: dry-run-by-default fires no HTTP call, --apply POSTs the expected writes body, non-users/ path rejected, malformed JSON rejected, 400 surfaces, array value accepted. Live: dry-run envelope + guard rejections confirmed.
